key_insights.insight: Many successful entrepreneurs are driven by a fear of regret, pushing them to take risks despite potential catastrophes.
key_insights.quote: It was reallyscary to leave his job at BCS, but it would be dangerous if he stayed because the danger would be that one day he'd get out of bed and he'd be 60 and he would regret not having tried.

key_insights.insight: Self-doubt is a common and healthy trait among entrepreneurs, but the key is the willingness to power through those doubts.
key_insights.quote: Most healthy entrepreneurs experience self-doubt. It is a really natural and I think important and necessary trait to be able to sort ofin your mind say, am I doing the right thing?

key_insights.insight: The ability to withstand rejection and hear 'no' repeatedly is a crucial skill for entrepreneurs.
key_insights.quote: The minute somebody says no to you is the minute the negotiation begins.

key_insights.insight: Some individuals may be more inclined toward entrepreneurship based on the environment in which they've grown (e.g., members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints).
key_insights.quote: And so when they go to start a business...they've heard no for so long on these missionsthat starting a business seems like a piece of cake because now they know that all they needto do is withstand those no's.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes, hitting 'rock bottom' or experiencing a significant setback is a necessary catalyst for change.
key_insights.quote: But they are often necessary to trigger the change that needs to happen.

key_insights.insight: Building a strong team around you is key to building a successful business, and often more valuable than individual brilliance.
key_insights.quote: Much of the job of a founderis to find these exceptional people and get the best out of them. That is like the easiest path to value creationversus hoping that you can become the best at designand computer programming and all of these kinds of things.

key_insights.insight: Culture is a shared belief in a common mission and values.
key_insights.quote: Strong culturesare places where people feel like they're working towards a common goal.

key_insights.insight: Knowing when to quit is a skill that winners have managed to really sharpen.
key_insights.quote: Butthe more people I've interviewed, the more I've come to learn that quitting is a bit of a skill.

key_insights.insight: Passion for solving a problem trumps the desire for money as a primary motivator for creating valuable things.
key_insights.quote: You can never, ever substitute passion with a desire for money.Passion is always going to trump a desire for money.

key_insights.insight: Mitigating risks instead of taking kamikaze leaps is the norm for most entrepreneurs.
key_insights.quote: But in general, most entrepreneurs calculate the risk. So they do take risks, but we all take risks every day. It's that they mitigate those risks in different ways.

actionable_advice.advice: Start your business slowly while still working a full-time job.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate a small percentage of your time to your business idea and gradually increase it as the business becomes more viable.

actionable_advice.advice: Focus on recruitment and building a strong team to maximize value creation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Reallocate your time to spend significantly more effort on finding and nurturing exceptional talent.

actionable_advice.advice: Define and codify family values to create a strong and supportive family culture.
actionable_advice.how_to: Hold regular family meetings to discuss and reinforce values like gratitude, generosity, and forgiveness.

memorable_stories: Jim Cook leaving a lucrative consulting job to start Samuel Adams brewery.
Isaac Larian's persistent pursuit of a licensing deal with Nintendo for Game & Watch.
Jason Worsland's invention of the Theragun after a motorcycle accident.
Monique Rodriguez starting Myel Organics hair care in her apartment and selling it to Procter & Gamble for half a billion dollars.
Hilary Swank's career revival after being written off Beverly Hills 90210 and winning an Oscar for Boys Don't Cry.
Tom Hank's early struggles with acting before starring in Bosom Buddies
contrarian_claims: Quitting is a skill, not a sign of failure, and it's essential for recognizing when to pivot.
Self-belief isn't always essential to start a successful business and self-doubt is actually a beneficial trait.
Entrepreneurs aren't kamikazes who take big risks without thinking. They calculate risk and mitigate them as best they can.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Extended Moment: Guy Raz: Why 99% of Businesses Fail & This Secret Made Apple, Pixar, & Elon Musk Billions!
